And that information, the information overwhelm meant, was getting in the way of my work and was getting in the way of my thinking. And so I essentially conceived of two lines of thinking. One, what if I could get. One hypothesis was if I were to just stream and filter. That's all it is, streaming, filtering, synthesizing. If I could, through a combination of streaming, filtering and synthesizing information from one place to the other, generate to a 80, 20 degree, a system that captures salient insights, hypotheses, etc. And builds those out, right? And so you could actually, through having the same idea sort of dispersed across multiple conversations, come to and discussed in many different ways, many angles, you can sort of average out what remains true throughout over a period of time, right? So can we set up a system where that happens and it sort of just rises to the top, like occurring, you know, so that was one hypothesis and then another. Another hypothesis was actually, you know, can I develop better cognitive principles? Because in a world of AI, it's very Easy to get, cognitive lazy. It's very easy to surrender control or even the lens you have on reality to AI, Right? And that is a dynamically opposed tension to the goal of streaming everything through AI. So how do you find, how do you reconcile these two ostensibly disparate goals? Well, that is where N5OS comes in, right? It is not cheap to run. I will be the first to say it is not cheap to run, but what it is is. It's not cheap to run, but what it is is essential. A set of like cognitive is simultaneously cognitive weighted jacket and cognitive guardrails, if that makes sense. It's meant to both stimulate thought in the ways that it's supposed to, while, you know, making things that should be easier and just sort of rote processing tasks easier and information organization and centralization tasks easier. Right, because ultimately a crucial part of peak performance is managing your cognitive load, right, and not getting cognitively overloaded. And so, yeah, that is sort of what N5OS is meant to be. And it's in that process that we develop both an AI that tries to, you know, have best practices like not glazing you, sort of being very objective, being doubtful, surfacing contradictions, surfacing areas of concern, etc. Etc. But also highly efficient at just going through my system and developing what is in essence developing what is in essence information consolidation engine and insight consolidation engine. So that is the value prop. And then obviously, for, I think for fairly obvious reasons, it's valuable to founders, right, who have to do a lot of these tasks, but also track original thinking, but also, also have that original thinking be placed within the context of the greater whole. So, yeah, those are my reflections on N5Os. I effectively want to turn this system that I build on Zoe into some, into a brand, into a sort of like, perspective of who I am. So I'm going to do a separate reflection around my personal brand, but I very much want this to be an element of my personal brand.
